info.title: Financial Datasets API — Macro Interest Rates
info.description: Central bank interest rate data (historical series + real-time snapshot), proxied through AIsa.

interest_rates.description: The current rate record for the requested bank. Returned as a list for shape consistency with the historical endpoint, typically holding the single latest observation.

bank.description: Code of the central bank this record belongs to, matching the `bank` you queried.

name.description: Full human-readable name of the central bank.

rate.description:
The bank's policy interest rate currently in effect.
[⚠️Note:源码未声明利率单位(百分数还是小数,如 5.25 还是 0.0525),待研发确认。]

date.description: Date this current rate took effect, in `YYYY-MM-DD`.

NotFoundError.description: The specified resource was not found

PaymentRequiredError.description: The request requires a paid subscription

example.message: This endpoint requires a paid subscription. Please upgrade your plan.

get.description:
Return the historical policy interest-rate series for a single central bank, optionally bounded to a date window. This is a read-only time-series query: omit the dates to get the full available history, or set a start and/or end to slice a range. Typical uses are charting rate trends and back-testing rate-sensitive models.

**Error status codes:** `400`, `401`, `402`, `404`

parameters.name: bank
parameters.description: Identifier of the central bank whose rate history to return, e.g. `FED` or `ECB`. The full list of accepted bank codes is available from the `/macro/interest-rates/banks` endpoint.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Lower bound of the date window, in `YYYY-MM-DD`. Records on or after this date are returned. Omit it to start from the earliest available record.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Upper bound of the date window, in `YYYY-MM-DD`. Records on or before this date are returned. Omit it to run through the most recent record.

get.description:
Return the current policy interest rate for a single central bank — the latest point in its rate series, not the full history. Use it when you only need the rate in effect right now, e.g. for a live dashboard or a one-off lookup.

**Error status codes:** `401`, `402`, `404`

parameters.name: bank
parameters.description: Identifier of the central bank whose current rate to return, e.g. `FED`, `ECB`, `BOJ`. The full list of accepted bank codes is available from the `/macro/interest-rates/banks` endpoint.
